behavior_summary: U8P_LAYGHOUL::look runs when an actor (typically the player) 'looks at' or examines a ghoul. The script causes the ghoul to emit a short bark containing the literal text "ghoul ".

At runtime the handler allocates/creates the literal string, sets per-process/process-info state (inferred from opcodes like 'set info' and 'process exclude'), pushes the viewer/process id and the string, invokes Item::bark on the ghoul object (the BP+06h parameter is used as the call receiver), frees the string, processes the bark return value with an 'implies' operation, then returns/suspends.
notable_control_flow: Entry on a 'U8P_LAYGHOUL::look' trigger → prepare string literal "ghoul " → set process/info-related flags (inferred) → push pid and string → call external Item::bark on the ghoul instance → free allocated string → push/consume return value with an 'implies' opcode → pop temporary and suspend/return.
There is no modification of global flags observed in this script fragment.
Several engine-level opcodes (set info, process exclude, implies) are present; their exact game-state semantics are inferred from pattern and are not fully canonical.
open_questions: Precise semantics of the 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes in this context — what exact per-object/process state is being modified and why?
Who exactly receives the bark? The pid pushed before the string suggests a target process/viewer, but the exact delivery rules of Item::bark (viewer-only vs. broadcast) are not shown here.
Meaning and effect of the 'implies' opcode on the return value from Item::bark — is this controlling conditional flow, event cancellation, or merely bookkeeping?

main_function.name: U8P_LAYGHOUL::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the 'U8P_LAYGHOUL::look' event on a ghoul by making that ghoul produce the text utterance "ghoul ".
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_LAYGHOUL::look) — invoked when the Avatar or another relevant actor looks at / examines the ghoul (enters the item's interaction region).

function_map.name: U8P_LAYGHOUL::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_LAYGHOUL::look' (examine) event for a ghoul object; cause it to emit a brief descriptive utterance.

external_calls.purpose: Ask the ghoul item/process to display/emit the provided text ("ghoul ") to the pushed target process/viewer; this implements the visible/audio feedback for examining the ghoul.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_LAYGHOUL::look runs when an actor (typically the player) 'looks at' or examines a ghoul. The script causes the ghoul to emit a short bark containing the literal text "ghoul ".
